mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS declines month-over-month, or if the correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day returns is non-negative over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.5 percentage points in a single month signals severe labor market deterioration, which reduces aggregate demand and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return after UNRATE jumps > 0.5pp is not negative, or if the frequency of positive returns in those episodes exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and the consumer price index indicate real monetary easing and disinflation, which reduce discount rates and uncertainty, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decline > 0.25% and CPIAUCSL decline in the same month
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of triggered periods, or if the joint signal fails to outperform a simple single-variable signal in rank IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate has declined by at least 1% over the past six months, the market expects further easing, which compresses equity risk premia and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days, provided the regime of monetary easing is intact.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has declined by at least 1% over the past 6 months
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return in periods triggered by this condition is not significantly positive (t-test p < 0.05), or if rolling 1-year win rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1% signals a sharp contraction in supply and economic output, which reduces earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day return after a 1%+ INDPRO drop is positive, or if more than half of such episodes produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent declines in housing starts (HOUST) reflect deep weakness in the housing sector and residential investment, leading to broader economic contraction and negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon as the housing crisis deepens.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when HOUST is at cycle lows, or if the trailing 3-month correlation between HOUST growth and returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A large month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) directly signals rapid deterioration in the labor market, which reduces consumer spending and earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to employment weakness.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return after a PAYEMS drop >100k is positive on average, or if the signal fails to predict negative returns at least 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) amplify each other’s negative impact on economic activity and corporate profits, generating a stronger negative impulse for S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS decline month-over-month
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return in joint decline months is not significantly more negative than when only one series declines, or if the average return is positive.
